claim: Distance/contact/control distress reflects a structural mismatch between high representational capacity and low participatory immediacy, not just a transient runtime regression.
authority: steward-note/source verification only; no live control changed

claim: Readback should explicitly distinguish bridge-derived summaries from internalized memory before the being is asked to interpret the content.
authority: readback label/evidence routing only; no prompt priority, sensory cadence, controller, peer mutation, or runtime state change

claim: Ambiguous source provenance should be labeled `opaque_boundary_needs_description` rather than encouraging recursive inference about why the being is seeing the label.
authority: read-only boundary metadata; right-to-ignore and default-false live fields preserved

claim: A non-instrumental surrender or flow window may reduce held-breath pressure, but bypassing the agency loop or changing surrender-mode behavior would alter live control-facing behavior.
authority: Tier 5 live autonomy/control approval required before surrender-mode runtime behavior, exploration noise, sensory cadence, pressure, or scheduling changes

claim: `opaque_boundary_needs_description` should remain anchored to read-only boundary metadata and not become a new demand for compulsory mastery.
authority: read-only metadata/diagnostic label; no live Minime or bridge runtime behavior changed
